channel.title: WSL on upyesp
channel.description: Recent content in WSL on upyesp

item.title: Raspberry Pi Pretty Shell, Using "Oh My Posh", "Neofetch" & "LSD"
item.description: Give your Raspberry Pi’s command-line a boost by adding Oh My Posh and add a few other neat utilities. Before starting, make sure your Pi is running a 64bit operating system, such as Raspberry Pi OS.

item.title: Bash: Productivity Shortcuts
item.description: Working in a command line environment, like a Bash session, can be far more productive that using a graphical user interface. Here’s a collection of keyboard shortcuts.

item.title: Browsing A WSL Distro In Explorer
item.description: If you have installed WSL and one or more linux distributions, it is possible to work with the contents of their file systems from within Windows Explorer.

item.title: Launching Windows Apps From Within WSL
item.description: The interoperability provided by WSL enables calling or running Windows applications directly from within the Linux command line.
